proposal_summary: Proposal to renew the Compound Grants Program (CGP) 2.0 for two quarters with a total budget of $970,000 (17,436.7 COMP), distributed across several domains and managed by Questbook using a Delegated Domain Capital Allocation Model.

disclosed_entities.name: Questbook
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: Questbook proposes to renew Compound Grants Program 2.0 with a budget of $970,000

disclosed_entities.name: Grants SAFE
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: Unallocated funds from the Grants SAFE and all domain level SAFE will be returned back to the treasury.

disclosed_entities.name: Synapse
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: We suggest that the grants committee continue with Synapse for KYC services

claimed_actions.raw_action: Renew Compound Grants Program 2.0 with a budget of 17,436.7 COMP
claimed_actions.claimed_effect: Fund grants that align with Compound’s roadmap and compensate program managers and domain allocators.
claimed_actions.textual_evidence: we propose renewing CGP 2.0 with a budget of $970k for two quarters. The domain allocators will utilize this budget to fund proposals that align with Compound’s roadmap.

claimed_complexity.reason: The proposal requires the transfer of treasury funds to multiple domain-level multisig wallets operated by a Grants committee.
claimed_complexity.textual_evidence: Each domain allocator will run their respective domain on-chain for full transparency using Questbook.

limitations: The proposal text specifies a budget and intent but lacks the explicit on-chain execution addresses or technical payload inside the main body text.
